stem: Which choice most effectively combines the information from the notes to achieve the student's goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Community gardens address food access issues while also fostering social connections and providing educational and health benefits to residents of all ages.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Community gardens provide fresh vegetables to local residents, and many neighborhoods lack access to affordable fresh produce.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Community gardens bring people together to work on shared projects, help reduce stress, and teach children about nutrition.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Fresh vegetables are available through community gardens, which also offer gardening activities that help reduce stress and improve mental health.

explanation: Choice A most effectively combines the information to emphasize the multiple benefits of community gardens. It connects the food access issue (addressing neighborhoods that lack affordable fresh produce) with the social benefits (fostering connections), health benefits (mental health improvement), and educational benefits (learning for children). The phrase 'residents of all ages' efficiently captures both adult and child beneficiaries mentioned in the notes. This choice creates a comprehensive overview that serves the student's goal of emphasizing the diverse advantages of community gardens.

distractorRationale.A: This is the correct answer as explained above.
distractorRationale.B: This choice only addresses two points from the notes (fresh vegetables and food access) and doesn't emphasize the multiple benefits the student wants to highlight. It also presents the information in a simple, disconnected way rather than showing how community gardens solve the access problem.
distractorRationale.C: While this choice mentions multiple benefits (social, health, and educational), it fails to address the important food access issue mentioned in the notes. It doesn't provide the comprehensive overview needed for an effective introduction.
distractorRationale.D: This choice focuses primarily on the fresh vegetables and health benefits but omits the social and educational aspects. It doesn't address the food access problem or show the full range of benefits the student wants to emphasize.

passage:
While researching for a report on community gardens, a student has gathered the following notes:

• Community gardens provide fresh vegetables to local residents
• Many neighborhoods lack access to affordable fresh produce
• These gardens bring people together to work on a shared project
• Gardening activities help reduce stress and improve mental health
• Children learn about nutrition and where food comes from

The student wants to write an introduction that emphasizes the multiple benefits of community gardens.
